description: Cross-platform G2P consistency test fixtures for Python, Rust, and JS runtimes. JA cases use structural checks (token_count_min) because OpenJTalk output varies by dictionary version. Rule-based languages (ES/FR/PT) can use exact expected_tokens. EN uses g2p-en which may vary across versions, so structural checks are preferred.

test_cases.description: Basic Japanese greeting — 5 morae, tests OpenJTalk pipeline

test_cases.input: 東京都に住んでいます。

detect_test_cases.description: CJK ideographs with katakana -> JA (kana presence disambiguates)

detect_test_cases.input: 今日はとても良い天気です。
detect_test_cases.description: Mixed CJK + hiragana sentence -> JA (kana disambiguates from ZH)
CJK ideographs without any kana -> ZH
Katakana + hiragana + kanji -> JA (katakana triggers kana detection)
Latin-only French text -> EN (default Latin; detector cannot distinguish Latin-script langs)
Mixed Japanese-English text -> JA (kana in を disambiguates CJK from ZH; Latin is minority)
